key_insight: A frozen CT-CLIP foundation model with a lightweight survival head can improve multimodal lung cancer survival prediction from pretreatment CT and clinical data.
clinical_relevance: This approach may help identify higher- and lower-risk lung cancer patients more accurately when curated imaging datasets are small.
method: Evaluated CT-CLIP as a feature extractor for pretreatment CT images.
Combined imaging representations with clinical variables for survival prediction.
Compared frozen encoders, full fine-tuning, LoRA, and modality ablations against clinical and multimodal baselines.
evidence_signal: The study analyzed 242 diagnosed lung cancer patients and found that frozen CT-CLIP with a trainable survival head outperformed the clinical baseline and was comparable to or better than other multimodal methods.
watch_item: Results come from a relatively small cohort, so broader validation is needed before real-world implementation.
paper_title: CT-CLIP Representations for Multimodal Lung Cancer Survival Prediction
